reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton expressed concern about the frequency of pre-existing issues found by Sashiko, specifically noting that four scans failed and requesting further investigation into what a full scan of mm/ would reveal.
reviews.raw_body:
And...  this made Sashiko point at other stuff:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260529172331.356655-1-kas@kernel.org

I can't figure out why four of the scans failed - "View Raw Log" comes up empty.

It's concerning how frequently Sashiko is finding pre-existing things.  What
would a full scan of mm/ tell us?  Gulp.

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch series addresses six pre-existing bugs in the userfaultfd and pagemap code, which were previously carried as part of a larger feature series. The issues include missing updates to hardware Dirty/Accessed bits, incorrect handling of gigantic hugetlb pages, self-deadlocks when scanning unpopulated hugetlb ranges, loss of uffd-wp markers during PMD permission downgrades, and out-of-bounds writes in the vma_flags_t structure. Each bug is fixed independently and applies to the mm-new code directly, with some patches also carrying Cc: stable@ for backporting. The approach taken involves correcting specific functions and data structures to ensure accurate and safe handling of userfaultfd and pagemap operations.

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau
reviews.summary: The patchset seems to be passed now, but there are concerns about potential conflicts and bandwidth for further review.

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand (Arm)
reviews.summary: Suspects a minor conflict with an incoming fix from Lorenzo and suggests putting the patch into mm-unstable early after the next release.

2026-06-01.patch_summary: The patchset is being held up due to pre-existing fixes and potential conflicts.
